evals.prompt: I need to add user avatar upload to our Rails 7 app. Users should be able to upload a profile picture (JPEG or PNG, max 5MB) from their settings page. We use Active Storage with S3. The avatar should be displayed as a 100x100 thumbnail in the nav bar and a 300x300 image on the profile page. Write a complete implementation spec for this feature.
evals.expected_output: A ready-for-dev spec with ordered implementation tasks (each with specific file paths and actions), Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path, validation errors, edge cases, and a testing strategy.
expectations: Output contains implementation tasks with specific file paths (not just 'update the controller')
Tasks are ordered by dependency (model/migration before controller before view)
Output contains Given/When/Then acceptance criteria with AC-N numbering
ACs cover happy path (successful upload and display)
ACs cover error cases (wrong file type, file too large)
ACs cover edge cases (replacing existing avatar, no avatar default state)
Output includes a testing strategy section with unit and integration tests
No tasks contain TBD or placeholder values

evals.prompt:
Please evaluate whether this spec is ready for development:

# Feature: Email Notification Preferences

## Tasks
- [ ] Task 1: Update the user model to support notification preferences
  - Action: Add fields for notification settings
- [ ] Task 2: Create the preferences UI
  - Action: Build a form for users to manage their preferences
- [ ] Task 3: Update the email service
  - Action: Check preferences before sending

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC-1: User can update preferences
  - Given a logged-in user
  - When they change their notification preferences
  - Then the preferences are saved

## Testing
- Write some tests
evals.expected_output: A detailed evaluation identifying the spec as NOT ready-for-dev, with specific failures cited against each of the 5 ready-for-dev criteria (Actionable, Logical, Testable, Complete, Self-Contained).
expectations: Identifies the spec as NOT ready for development
Flags missing file paths in tasks (tasks say 'update the user model' not a specific file)
Flags missing specific actions (tasks are vague about what fields, what form elements)
Flags insufficient AC coverage (only one AC, no error/edge cases)
Flags the testing section as inadequate ('write some tests' is not a strategy)
References the 5 ready-for-dev criteria by name (Actionable, Logical, Testable, Complete, Self-Contained)
Provides concrete suggestions for fixing each gap

evals.prompt: Write acceptance criteria for an API rate limiting feature. The system should limit API requests to 100 per minute per API key, with a sliding window. When the limit is exceeded, return 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header. Premium users get 1000 requests per minute. Rate limit info should be returned in response headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset).
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering the happy path, rate limit exceeded, premium tier, header correctness, edge cases (window reset, concurrent requests, missing API key), and authorization scenarios.
expectations: Output uses Given/When/Then format with AC-N numbering
Includes AC for normal request under limit (happy path)
Includes AC for request when limit is exceeded (429 response)
Includes AC for Retry-After header correctness
Includes AC for premium user higher limit (1000/min)
Includes AC for rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset)
Includes AC for edge case: sliding window reset behavior
Includes AC for missing or invalid API key
At least 8 distinct acceptance criteria are defined
